C64.bio: Dexion's founding musician on the SID chip. Future Freak's distinctive arpeggios and driving basslines defined the group's audio identity throughout the C64 years and well into the Amiga era.

Founding member and organizer of Danish Demo Duo. Coder and swapper, switch to Amiga in 1987 and joined Dexion in 1988. Pet's 68000 assembly skills were instrumental in the development of the group's first Amiga release.
The technical backbone of Dexion's C64 era. Starman pioneered raster tricks, sprite multiplexing, and the custom interrupt routines that became group staples across dozens of releases.
Founder of Dexion's. The Tiger was the first C64 swapper to join the group.
Founding member and organizer of Danish Demo Duo. Brilliant coder and graphic-wizard. Joined Dexion in 1988 together with Pet
The longest-serving artist in the group. Pixel mastered character-set art and sprite design on C64 before transitioning to Amiga HAM and AGA modes, bridging both eras of Dexion history.

Amiga.bio: Did the first Amiga demo. Dr. Dolby's music was the driving force behind the group's success in the Amiga era, and was instrumental in the development of the group's first Amiga release.
Brought a distinctly European electronic influence to the Amiga section. Future Freak's tracks layered complex polyrhythms with melodic hooks that were unusual for the era.
Brought a distinctly European electronic influence to the Amiga section. Future Freak's tracks layered complex polyrhythms with melodic hooks that were unusual for the era.
Part-coder on the first Megademo. Liteace specialised in audio replay routines and hardware-level interrupt management, ensuring perfect music synchronisation across hardware variants.
Co-author of Dexion Megademo 1. Mr. Mega Mind contributed scroll routines, part frameworks, and the fast loader system that was reused across multiple subsequent releases.
The visual powerhouse of the Amiga section. Art Attack painted full-screen pictures, logos, and transitions that pushed the Amiga palette to its absolute limits on both OCS and AGA hardware.

Producer of a lot of great ProTracker modules. Omen's music was the driving force behind the group's success in the Amiga era, and was instrumental in the development of the group's first Amiga release.
Brilliant coder and great at physics and mathematics. Orbit's 68000 assembly skills were instrumental in the development of the group's first Amiga release.
Danish coder known for compact, highly optimised 68000 assembly routines. Pet contributed the copper plasma and smooth-scrolling effects in Megademo 1 before departing to work solo.
Founder of Dexion's. The Tiger was the first Amiga swapper to join the group.
Early Amiga artist who designed the iconic Dexion logo seen in Megademo 1's intro screen. Tox retired from the scene in 1990 to pursue a career in commercial graphic design.
Danish coder known for compact, highly optimised 68000 assembly routines.
